sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin k at 71% daily value, vitamin e at 12% daily value stand out most. that gives this food real vitamin support.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side: can help meals feel more complete and usable. fats sometimes improve the whole plate, not just their own stats. highly useful in cooking. practical culinary value matters here. small amounts can change a whole meal. that leverage matters when the fat quality is good.

sections.subtitleText: on the plus side: can help meals feel more complete and usable. fats sometimes improve the whole plate, not just their own stats. highly useful in cooking. practical culinary value matters here. small amounts can change a whole meal. that leverage matters when the fat quality is good.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: easy to overpour without noticing. practical portion creep is real. extremely calorie dense by default. that is the unavoidable category burden. not a complete food on its own. even the better fats are still just part of a meal.

closing.summary: Canola Oil has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.useCaseNote: Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while vitamins are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
